title: Fix resuming after `ds.set_epoch(new_epoch)`
body:
## Title
Fix resuming after `ds.set_epoch(new_epoch)`

## Description
close https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/issues/7447

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fix deterministic resumption after ds.set_epoch(new_epoch) by ensuring epoch changes propagate to the prepared iteration pipeline and that resume from state_dict respects the current epoch context, across workers and distributed shuffles.

steps.operation: Refactor _effective_generator to derive RNG seed strictly from the current epoch (and shuffle/distributed flags) to ensure deterministic shuffling across set_epoch changes and resume scenarios
steps.reason: To guarantee robust, epoch-driven RNG seeding even when resuming from checkpoints or when shuffling is toggled, ensuring consistency across workers/processes
steps.side_effects: May alter shuffling behavior across all DataLoader consumers; requires downstream tests and potential re-seeding of any dependent wrappers

steps.operation: Invalidate or refresh the prepared ex_iterable cache when set_epoch is called
steps.reason: To ensure subsequent iterations rebuild the ex_iterable stack using the new epoch seed, preventing stale RNG seeds from persisting after a change in epoch
steps.side_effects: Triggers reinitialization of data pipelines; may introduce a brief overhead during epoch changes; affects any component relying on cached ex_iterable (e.g., DataLoader workers)

steps.operation: Rebuild the ex_iterable stack using the current epoch in _prepare_ex_iterable_for_iteration
steps.reason: To guarantee that shuffles, wrappers (shuffle/map/batch), and distributed sharding are seeded with the current epoch, including after set_epoch calls or resume
steps.side_effects: Changes initialization path of the iteration pipeline; requires validation across various wrapper combinations and potential performance implications; tests must cover epoch consistency after rebuild

steps.operation: Enhance load_state_dict to respect the current epoch context and rebind iterables after resume
steps.reason: To avoid resuming with a stale or mismatched RNG seed by aligning the resumed state with the active epoch (or explicitly restoring epoch from saved state), and re-binding the underlying iterables accordingly
steps.side_effects: Might require embedding or handling epoch metadata in the saved state_dict; impacts resume paths and tests for checkpointing across epochs; could affect compatibility with older saved states
